en.h1: How to Review a Contract Without Being a Lawyer
en.sub: Most contracts are 80% boilerplate and 20% things that actually matter. The problem is knowing which 20%. A practical process for reviewing any vendor contract in under an hour.
en.intro: Sending every contract to a lawyer costs $300–$800 per review and takes 3–5 days. Most teams skip the review entirely. This guide gives you a structured process you can run yourself.
en.h2a: Step 1: Read the Summary Terms First
en.s1p: Before reading any legal text, locate: price, term length, renewal clause, and termination rights. These four determine 80% of the financial risk. If the renewal is automatic and the notice window is under 60 days, flag it immediately.
en.p2: Use Ctrl+F to search for these phrases. Each one is a prompt to read the surrounding paragraph carefully:
en.p2b: These phrases don't automatically mean the clause is bad — but they're almost always worth reading carefully. A "sole discretion" clause that governs pricing is very different from one that governs feature prioritization.
en.s2p: A 20-page contract read linearly takes 45 minutes. The same contract reviewed with targeted keyword searches takes 10 minutes and often catches more.
en.h2c: Step 3: Check the Four High-Risk Sections
en.s3p: These four sections contain the majority of financial risk. Read each one completely. The indemnification section often contains liability not in the 'Limitation of Liability' section.
en.s4p: For every obligation you take on, ask: does the other party have the same obligation? Asymmetric clauses in liability, indemnification, and termination rights are the most common structural problems.
en.wt: Go through each key clause and ask: 'what would happen if I swapped the parties?' If the result sounds unfair, the clause is asymmetric. Vendors modify these far more often than buyers realize.
en.h2e: Step 5: Build Your Short List of Asks
en.s5p: Identify your top 3 asks — the clauses with the highest financial risk — and lead with those. A focused ask signals a serious buyer and gets a faster response than a 15-point redline.
en.h2f: When to Actually Call a Lawyer
en.p_lawyer: This process handles 90% of vendor contract reviews. But call a lawyer when:
en.l2: You're granting IP rights or a license to your product/data
en.l3: The contract is governed by a foreign jurisdiction
en.l4: Indemnification obligations are unusually broad or the liability cap is missing entirely
en.p_final: For everything else, a structured review combined with an AI analysis gives you enough visibility to negotiate confidently.
en.ctah: Want the analysis done in 2 minutes instead of 45?
en.ctap: Upload your contract to Conditio. We flag high-risk clauses, score overall risk, and give a plain-English summary — so you know exactly what to push back on.

es.h1: Cómo Revisar un Contrato Sin Ser Abogado
es.sub: La mayoría de los contratos son 80% boilerplate y 20% lo que realmente importa. El problema es saber cuál es ese 20%. Un proceso práctico para revisar cualquier contrato en menos de una hora.
es.intro: Enviar cada contrato a un abogado cuesta $300-$800 por revisión y toma 3-5 días. La mayoría de los equipos se saltean la revisión por completo. Esta guía te da un proceso estructurado que puedes ejecutar tú mismo.
es.h2a: Paso 1: Lee Primero los Términos Clave
es.s1p: Antes de leer el texto legal, localiza: precio, duración, cláusula de renovación y derechos de rescisión. Estos cuatro determinan el 80% del riesgo financiero.
es.h2b: Paso 2: Busca Palabras Clave de Peligro
es.p2: Usa Ctrl+F para buscar estas frases. Cada una es un aviso para leer el párrafo circundante con cuidado:
es.p2b: Estas frases no significan automáticamente que la cláusula sea mala — pero casi siempre vale la pena leerlas con cuidado.
es.s2p: Un contrato de 20 páginas leído linealmente lleva 45 minutos. El mismo contrato revisado con búsquedas de palabras clave lleva 10 minutos.
es.h2c: Paso 3: Revisa las Cuatro Secciones de Alto Riesgo
es.s3p: Estas cuatro secciones contienen la mayoría del riesgo financiero. Lee cada una completamente.
es.s4p: Para cada obligación que asumes, pregunta: ¿tiene la otra parte la misma obligación? Las cláusulas asimétricas en responsabilidad e indemnización son los problemas estructurales más comunes.
es.wt: Revisa cada cláusula clave y pregunta: '¿qué pasaría si intercambiara a las partes?' Si el resultado suena injusto, la cláusula es asimétrica.
es.h2e: Paso 5: Construye tu Lista Corta de Peticiones
es.s5p: Identifica tus 3 peticiones principales — las cláusulas con mayor riesgo financiero — y empieza por esas.
es.h2f: Cuándo Realmente Llamar a un Abogado
es.p_lawyer: Este proceso maneja el 90% de las revisiones de contratos. Pero llama a un abogado cuando:
es.l1: El valor del contrato supera $50,000 anuales
es.l2: Estás otorgando derechos de PI o una licencia sobre tu producto/datos
es.l3: El contrato está regido por una jurisdicción extranjera
es.l4: Las obligaciones de indemnización son inusualmente amplias o falta completamente el límite de responsabilidad
es.p_final: Para todo lo demás, una revisión estructurada combinada con análisis de IA te da suficiente visibilidad para negociar con confianza.
es.ctah: ¿Quieres el análisis en 2 minutos en vez de 45?
es.ctap: Sube tu contrato a Conditio. Señalamos las cláusulas de alto riesgo y te damos un resumen en lenguaje claro.

fr.h1: Comment Relire un Contrat Sans Être Avocat
fr.sub: La plupart des contrats sont 80% de clauses standard et 20% qui comptent vraiment. Le problème est de savoir lesquels. Un processus pratique en moins d'une heure.
fr.intro: Envoyer chaque contrat à un avocat coûte 300-800€ par révision et prend 3-5 jours. La plupart des équipes sautent la révision. Ce guide vous donne un processus structuré que vous pouvez suivre vous-même.
fr.h2a: Étape 1: Lire d'abord les termes essentiels
fr.s1p: Avant de lire le texte juridique, repérez: prix, durée, clause de renouvellement, droits de résiliation. Ces quatre éléments déterminent 80% du risque financier.
fr.p2: Utilisez Ctrl+F pour rechercher ces phrases:
fr.p2b: Ces phrases ne signifient pas automatiquement que la clause est mauvaise — mais elles méritent presque toujours une lecture attentive.
fr.s2p: Un contrat de 20 pages lu linéairement prend 45 minutes. Le même avec des recherches ciblées prend 10 minutes.
fr.h2c: Étape 3: Vérifier les quatre sections à haut risque
fr.s3p: Ces quatre sections contiennent la majorité du risque financier. Lisez chacune en entier.
fr.s4p: Pour chaque obligation que vous assumez, demandez: l'autre partie a-t-elle la même? Les clauses asymétriques sont les problèmes structurels les plus courants.
fr.wt: Pour chaque clause clé, demandez-vous: que se passerait-il si j'échangeais les parties? Si le résultat semble injuste, la clause est asymétrique.
fr.h2e: Étape 5: Établir votre liste courte de demandes
fr.s5p: Identifiez vos 3 demandes principales — les clauses à plus haut risque financier — et commencez par celles-là.
fr.p_lawyer: Ce processus couvre 90% des révisions. Mais appelez un avocat quand:
fr.l1: La valeur du contrat dépasse 50 000€ annuels
fr.l2: Vous accordez des droits de PI ou une licence sur votre produit/données
fr.l3: Le contrat est régi par une juridiction étrangère
fr.l4: Les obligations d'indemnisation sont inhabituellement larges ou le plafond de responsabilité est absent
fr.p_final: Pour tout le reste, une révision structurée combinée à une analyse IA vous donne une visibilité suffisante pour négocier en confiance.
fr.ctah: Vous voulez l'analyse en 2 minutes au lieu de 45?
fr.ctap: Téléchargez votre contrat sur Conditio. Nous signalons les clauses à risque élevé et vous fournissons un résumé en langage clair.

de.h1: Wie man einen Vertrag prüft ohne Anwalt zu sein
de.sub: Die meisten Verträge sind 80% Standardklauseln und 20% wirklich wichtige Inhalte. Das Problem ist zu wissen, welche 20%. Ein praktischer Prozess für unter einer Stunde.
de.intro: Jeden Vertrag zum Anwalt zu schicken kostet 300-800€ pro Prüfung und dauert 3-5 Tage. Die meisten Teams überspringen die Prüfung. Dieser Leitfaden gibt Ihnen einen strukturierten Prozess.
de.s1p: Vor dem Lesen des Rechtstexts: Preis, Laufzeit, Verlängerungsklausel und Kündigungsrechte. Diese vier bestimmen 80% des finanziellen Risikos.
de.p2: Verwenden Sie Strg+F um nach diesen Phrasen zu suchen:
de.p2b: Diese Phrasen bedeuten nicht automatisch, dass die Klausel schlecht ist — aber sie lohnen sich fast immer genauer zu lesen.
de.s2p: Ein 20-seitiger Vertrag linear gelesen dauert 45 Minuten. Mit gezielten Suchbegriffen dauert es 10 Minuten.
de.s3p: Diese vier Abschnitte enthalten den Großteil des finanziellen Risikos. Lesen Sie jeden vollständig.
de.s4p: Für jede Verpflichtung, die Sie eingehen, fragen Sie: hat die andere Partei dieselbe? Asymmetrische Klauseln sind die häufigsten strukturellen Probleme.
de.wt: Fragen Sie bei jeder Klausel: Was wäre, wenn ich die Parteien tauschen würde? Wenn das Ergebnis unfair klingt, ist die Klausel asymmetrisch.
de.s5h: Versuchen Sie nicht, alles zu beheben
de.s5p: Identifizieren Sie Ihre 3 wichtigsten Anfragen — die Klauseln mit dem höchsten finanziellen Risiko. Fokussierte Anfragen erhalten schnellere Antworten.
de.h2f: Wann man wirklich einen Anwalt anruft
de.p_lawyer: Dieser Prozess behandelt 90% der Vertragsüberprüfungen. Aber rufen Sie einen Anwalt an, wenn:
de.l2: Sie IP-Rechte oder eine Lizenz an Ihrem Produkt/Daten gewähren
de.l3: Der Vertrag einer ausländischen Rechtsordnung unterliegt
de.l4: Die Freistellungspflichten ungewöhnlich weit sind oder der Haftungsdeckel vollständig fehlt
de.p_final: Für alles andere gibt Ihnen eine strukturierte Prüfung kombiniert mit KI-Analyse genug Einblick, um selbstbewusst zu verhandeln.
de.ctah: Möchten Sie die Analyse in 2 Minuten statt 45?
de.ctap: Laden Sie Ihren Vertrag auf Conditio hoch. Wir markieren Hochrisikoklauseln und geben eine Zusammenfassung in einfacher Sprache.

it.h1: Come Revisionare un Contratto Senza Essere Avvocato
it.sub: La maggior parte dei contratti è 80% boilerplate e 20% cose che contano davvero. Il problema è sapere qual è quel 20%. Un processo pratico per rivedere qualsiasi contratto in meno di un'ora.
it.intro: Inviare ogni contratto a un avvocato costa 300-800€ per revisione e richiede 3-5 giorni. La maggior parte dei team salta la revisione. Questa guida ti offre un processo strutturato.
it.h2a: Passo 1: Leggi prima i termini essenziali
it.s1p: Prima di leggere il testo legale, individua: prezzo, durata, clausola di rinnovo e diritti di rescissione. Questi quattro determinano l'80% del rischio finanziario.
it.h2b: Passo 2: Cerca le parole chiave di pericolo
it.p2: Usa Ctrl+F per cercare queste frasi:
it.p2b: Queste frasi non significano automaticamente che la clausola sia cattiva — ma quasi sempre vale la pena leggerle attentamente.
it.s2p: Un contratto di 20 pagine letto linearmente richiede 45 minuti. Lo stesso con ricerche mirate ne richiede 10.
it.h2c: Passo 3: Controlla le quattro sezioni ad alto rischio
it.s3p: Queste quattro sezioni contengono la maggior parte del rischio finanziario. Leggile completamente.
it.s4p: Per ogni obbligo che assumi, chiedi: ha la stessa obbligo l'altra parte? Le clausole asimmetriche in responsabilità e indennizzo sono i problemi strutturali più comuni.
it.wt: Per ogni clausola chiave chiedi: cosa succederebbe se scambiassi le parti? Se il risultato suona ingiusto, la clausola è asimmetrica.
it.h2e: Passo 5: Costruisci la tua lista breve di richieste
it.s5p: Identifica le tue 3 richieste principali — le clausole con il rischio finanziario più alto — e inizia da quelle.
it.p_lawyer: Questo processo gestisce il 90% delle revisioni. Ma chiama un avvocato quando:
it.l1: Il valore del contratto supera 50.000€ annui
it.l2: Stai concedendo diritti di PI o una licenza sul tuo prodotto/dati
it.l3: Il contratto è regolato da una giurisdizione straniera
it.l4: Gli obblighi di indennizzo sono insolitamente ampi o il limite di responsabilità è completamente assente
it.p_final: Per tutto il resto, una revisione strutturata combinata con analisi AI ti dà abbastanza visibilità per negoziare con sicurezza.
it.ctah: Vuoi l'analisi in 2 minuti invece di 45?
it.ctap: Carica il tuo contratto su Conditio. Segnaliamo le clausole ad alto rischio e forniamo un riepilogo in linguaggio semplice.

pt.h1: Como Rever um Contrato Sem Ser Advogado
pt.sub: A maioria dos contratos é 80% cláusulas padrão e 20% coisas que realmente importam. O problema é saber quais. Um processo prático para rever qualquer contrato em menos de uma hora.
pt.intro: Enviar cada contrato a um advogado custa 300-800€ por revisão e demora 3-5 dias. A maioria das equipas ignora a revisão. Este guia dá-lhe um processo estruturado.
pt.h2a: Passo 1: Leia primeiro os termos essenciais
pt.s1p: Antes de ler o texto jurídico, localize: preço, duração, cláusula de renovação e direitos de rescisão. Estes quatro determinam 80% do risco financeiro.
pt.p2: Use Ctrl+F para procurar estas frases:
pt.p2b: Estas frases não significam automaticamente que a cláusula é má — mas quase sempre vale a pena ler com cuidado.
pt.s2p: Um contrato de 20 páginas lido linearmente demora 45 minutos. O mesmo com pesquisas direcionadas demora 10 minutos.
pt.h2c: Passo 3: Verifique as quatro secções de alto risco
pt.s3p: Estas quatro secções contêm a maioria do risco financeiro. Leia cada uma completamente.
pt.s4p: Para cada obrigação que assume, pergunte: a outra parte tem a mesma obrigação? Cláusulas assimétricas em responsabilidade e indemnização são os problemas estruturais mais comuns.
pt.wt: Para cada cláusula chave, pergunte: o que aconteceria se trocasse as partes? Se o resultado parece injusto, a cláusula é assimétrica.
pt.h2e: Passo 5: Construa a sua lista curta de pedidos
pt.s5p: Identifique os seus 3 pedidos principais — as cláusulas com maior risco financeiro — e comece por esses.
pt.h2f: Quando Realmente Ligar a um Advogado
pt.p_lawyer: Este processo cobre 90% das revisões. Mas ligue a um advogado quando:
pt.l1: O valor do contrato excede 50.000€ anuais
pt.l2: Está a conceder direitos de PI ou uma licença sobre o seu produto/dados
pt.l3: O contrato é regido por uma jurisdição estrangeira
pt.l4: As obrigações de indemnização são invulgarmente amplas ou o limite de responsabilidade está completamente ausente
pt.p_final: Para tudo o resto, uma revisão estruturada combinada com análise de IA dá-lhe visibilidade suficiente para negociar com confiança.
pt.ctah: Quer a análise feita em 2 minutos em vez de 45?
pt.ctap: Carregue o seu contrato no Conditio. Assinalamos as cláusulas de alto risco e fornecemos um resumo em linguagem clara.

en-gb.h1: How to Review a Contract Without Being a Lawyer
en-gb.sub: Most contracts are 80% boilerplate and 20% things that actually matter. The problem is knowing which 20%. A practical process for reviewing any vendor contract in under an hour.
en-gb.intro: Sending every contract to a lawyer costs $300–$800 per review and takes 3–5 days. Most teams skip the review entirely. This guide gives you a structured process you can run yourself.
en-gb.h2a: Step 1: Read the Summary Terms First
en-gb.s1p: Before reading any legal text, locate: price, term length, renewal clause, and termination rights. These four determine 80% of the financial risk. If the renewal is automatic and the notice window is under 60 days, flag it immediately.
en-gb.p2: Use Ctrl+F to search for these phrases. Each one is a prompt to read the surrounding paragraph carefully:
en-gb.p2b: These phrases don't automatically mean the clause is bad — but they're almost always worth reading carefully. A "sole discretion" clause that governs pricing is very different from one that governs feature prioritization.
en-gb.s2p: A 20-page contract read linearly takes 45 minutes. The same contract reviewed with targeted keyword searches takes 10 minutes and often catches more.
en-gb.h2c: Step 3: Check the Four High-Risk Sections
en-gb.s3p: These four sections contain the majority of financial risk. Read each one completely. The indemnification section often contains liability not in the 'Limitation of Liability' section.
en-gb.s4p: For every obligation you take on, ask: does the other party have the same obligation? Asymmetric clauses in liability, indemnification, and termination rights are the most common structural problems.
en-gb.wt: Go through each key clause and ask: 'what would happen if I swapped the parties?' If the result sounds unfair, the clause is asymmetric. Vendors modify these far more often than buyers realize.
en-gb.h2e: Step 5: Build Your Short List of Asks
en-gb.s5p: Identify your top 3 asks — the clauses with the highest financial risk — and lead with those. A focused ask signals a serious buyer and gets a faster response than a 15-point redline.
en-gb.h2f: When to Actually Call a Lawyer
en-gb.p_lawyer: This process handles 90% of vendor contract reviews. But call a lawyer when:
en-gb.l2: You're granting IP rights or a license to your product/data
en-gb.l3: The contract is governed by a foreign jurisdiction
en-gb.l4: Indemnification obligations are unusually broad or the liability cap is missing entirely
en-gb.p_final: For everything else, a structured review combined with an AI analysis gives you enough visibility to negotiate confidently.
en-gb.ctah: Want the analysis done in 2 minutes instead of 45?
en-gb.ctap: Upload your contract to Conditio. We flag high-risk clauses, score overall risk, and give a plain-English summary — so you know exactly what to push back on.

es-latam.l1: El valor del contrato supera $50,000 anuales
es-latam.l2: Estás otorgando derechos de PI o una licencia sobre tu producto/datos
es-latam.l3: El contrato está regido por una jurisdicción extranjera
es-latam.l4: Las obligaciones de indemnización son inusualmente amplias o falta completamente el límite de responsabilidad
es-latam.p_final: Para todo lo demás, una revisión estructurada combinada con análisis de IA te da suficiente visibilidad para negociar con confianza.
es-latam.ctah: ¿Quieres el análisis en 2 minutos en vez de 45?
es-latam.ctap: Sube tu contrato a Conditio. Señalamos las cláusulas de alto riesgo y te damos un resumen en lenguaje claro.

nl.h1: How to Review a Contract Without Being a Lawyer
nl.sub: Most contracts are 80% boilerplate and 20% things that actually matter. The problem is knowing which 20%. A practical process for reviewing any vendor contract in under an hour.
nl.intro: Sending every contract to a lawyer costs $300–$800 per review and takes 3–5 days. Most teams skip the review entirely. This guide gives you a structured process you can run yourself.
nl.h2a: Step 1: Read the Summary Terms First
nl.s1p: Before reading any legal text, locate: price, term length, renewal clause, and termination rights. These four determine 80% of the financial risk. If the renewal is automatic and the notice window is under 60 days, flag it immediately.
nl.p2: Use Ctrl+F to search for these phrases. Each one is a prompt to read the surrounding paragraph carefully:
nl.p2b: These phrases don't automatically mean the clause is bad — but they're almost always worth reading carefully. A "sole discretion" clause that governs pricing is very different from one that governs feature prioritization.
nl.s2p: A 20-page contract read linearly takes 45 minutes. The same contract reviewed with targeted keyword searches takes 10 minutes and often catches more.
nl.h2c: Step 3: Check the Four High-Risk Sections
nl.s3p: These four sections contain the majority of financial risk. Read each one completely. The indemnification section often contains liability not in the 'Limitation of Liability' section.
nl.s4p: For every obligation you take on, ask: does the other party have the same obligation? Asymmetric clauses in liability, indemnification, and termination rights are the most common structural problems.
nl.wt: Go through each key clause and ask: 'what would happen if I swapped the parties?' If the result sounds unfair, the clause is asymmetric. Vendors modify these far more often than buyers realize.
nl.h2e: Step 5: Build Your Short List of Asks
nl.s5p: Identify your top 3 asks — the clauses with the highest financial risk — and lead with those. A focused ask signals a serious buyer and gets a faster response than a 15-point redline.
nl.h2f: When to Actually Call a Lawyer
nl.p_lawyer: This process handles 90% of vendor contract reviews. But call a lawyer when:
nl.l2: You're granting IP rights or a license to your product/data
nl.l3: The contract is governed by a foreign jurisdiction
nl.l4: Indemnification obligations are unusually broad or the liability cap is missing entirely
nl.p_final: For everything else, a structured review combined with an AI analysis gives you enough visibility to negotiate confidently.
nl.ctah: Want the analysis done in 2 minutes instead of 45?
nl.ctap: Upload your contract to Conditio. We flag high-risk clauses, score overall risk, and give a plain-English summary — so you know exactly what to push back on.

zh.h2c: Step 3: Check the Four High-Risk Sections
zh.s3p: These four sections contain the majority of financial risk. Read each one completely. The indemnification section often contains liability not in the 'Limitation of Liability' section.
zh.s4p: For every obligation you take on, ask: does the other party have the same obligation? Asymmetric clauses in liability, indemnification, and termination rights are the most common structural problems.
zh.wt: Go through each key clause and ask: 'what would happen if I swapped the parties?' If the result sounds unfair, the clause is asymmetric. Vendors modify these far more often than buyers realize.
zh.h2e: Step 5: Build Your Short List of Asks
zh.s5p: Identify your top 3 asks — the clauses with the highest financial risk — and lead with those. A focused ask signals a serious buyer and gets a faster response than a 15-point redline.
zh.h2f: When to Actually Call a Lawyer
zh.p_lawyer: This process handles 90% of vendor contract reviews. But call a lawyer when:
zh.l2: You're granting IP rights or a license to your product/data
zh.l3: The contract is governed by a foreign jurisdiction
zh.l4: Indemnification obligations are unusually broad or the liability cap is missing entirely
zh.p_final: For everything else, a structured review combined with an AI analysis gives you enough visibility to negotiate confidently.
zh.ctah: Want the analysis done in 2 minutes instead of 45?
zh.ctap: Upload your contract to Conditio. We flag high-risk clauses, score overall risk, and give a plain-English summary — so you know exactly what to push back on.
